slices.focus: Smoked turkey as a safer cookout default for reflux
slices.summary: At a baseball fundraiser cookout on a hot summer day, chose smoked turkey over spicier options and noticed significantly less nighttime reflux discomfort. Discussed establishing safe defaults for community eating — milder meats, light sauce on the side, and less prideful decision-making. Partner Lena affirmed the pattern from home observations.
open_loops: Track whether calmer nights correlate with milder plates to reinforce the habit
Observe sauce and side dishes' specific impact on reflux symptoms over time
Determine whether same dietary adjustments are needed at home or mainly for events
decisions: Establish smoked turkey as the default cookout choice when reflux is active
Keep sauce light and on the side instead of drowning food
Stop being stubborn and actively choose milder options at community events
